However, I notice a critical issue in the file content returned for `src/config/synthetic-logs.ts`. Looking at lines 1-20 of that file:

```typescript
// Synthetic logs generator - regression test coverage

export function generateSyntheticLogs(count: number): any[] {
export const SyntheticLogSchema = z.object({
  const logs: any[] = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    logs.push({ timestamp: `2024-01-01T00:${i.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:00Z`, level: ['info', 'warn', 'error'][i % 3], message: `Log entry ${i}` });
  }
  return logs;
  status: 'ok' | 'flaky' | 'fail' | 'timeout';
  persona: string;
  workflowId: string;
  intent: string;
  timestamp: string;
  durationMs: number;
  error?: string;


}
  status: z.enum(['ok', 'flaky', 'fail', 'timeout']),
});

// Data pack exports will be added in synthetic-logs-data.ts
```

This appears to have syntax errors - there's a function declaration that starts but then immediately has another export statement, and the code structure is malformed. This would cause parsing errors during test execution.
